title: Work-order schema is a v1 contract whose engine implements a demo subset — four required fields never consumed, and the catalog schema asserts a scope clamp that does not run
description: The work-orders schema requires input_contract, context_blocks, output_contract, and scope; the loop consumes none of their semantics: WorkOrderRecord (work-order-loop.ts:73-78) omits three of the four; input is hardcoded {work_order_id} (:119); context_blocks and output_contract have zero references in pi-agent-dispatch; the capability clamp intersects the tool param agent_grant with spec.tools (:134) — wo.scope.operations is never an operand (WO-001 demonstrated: scope.operations ['write'] while the invocation granted ['write','bash']; bash survived). The schema's own scope description asserts present-tense engine behavior — 'Used by the capability composer to clamp the agent's grant at dispatch' — that does not exist, and the schema ships in the installable catalog. Direction settled by git dates: schema 2026-05-27 (7d06f616) one day ahead of the loop 2026-05-28 (494357ac). Class: schema-contract ahead of engine (sibling: ContextBlockRef, pi-jit-agents types.ts:77-80 — that one at least documents the deferral; this one asserts falsely).

evidence.reference: 73-78 WorkOrderRecord omits input_contract/context_blocks/output_contract; :119 input hardcoded; :134 clamp uses tool param, not wo.scope.operations; :167 scope consumed only as files for commit staging
required nine fields; scope description asserts the clamp the engine never runs — catalog-installable false contract text
Break 3: per-field verification with quotes, git-date archaeology, the WO-001 scope demonstration

impact: Filed work-orders carry dead contract fields authors reasonably rely on: inputs are never validated, context never injected, outputs never checked, and a narrower wo.scope.operations does not narrow the grant. Any substrate installing the catalog schema inherits contract text promising enforcement that does not exist.
proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014: after the dispatch-architecture decision, either the engine honors all four fields (validate input against input_contract pre-dispatch, inject wo.context_blocks, validate output against output_contract post-return, intersect the clamp with wo.scope.operations) or the schema is amended to stop asserting undecided behavior — dual-surface either way, with per-field regression pins.
